title: Implement App-Open-to-Most-Recent-Conversation Logic

acceptance_criteria.description: User has 5 conversations, the most recent updated 2 minutes ago - App launches - Loading indicator shows briefly, then app navigates to the most recently updated conversation's chat screen
acceptance_criteria.notes: Uses hasInitialized ref to prevent duplicate execution, shows InitialLoadingScreen during initial fetch

acceptance_criteria.description: User has no conversations (first launch or all deleted) - App launches - Loading indicator shows, a new 'New Chat' conversation is created in Supabase, app navigates to it
acceptance_criteria.notes: If conversations.length === 0, calls createConversation() and navigates to new conversation

acceptance_criteria.description: App is loading conversations from Supabase - Initial fetch is in progress - A loading indicator (spinner or skeleton) is displayed, no blank screen or flash of content
acceptance_criteria.notes: InitialLoadingScreen component with ActivityIndicator and loading message

acceptance_criteria.description: Supabase fetch fails on app launch - Network error or invalid credentials - Error state is displayed with a 'Retry' button, user can tap to retry the initial fetch
acceptance_criteria.notes: InitialErrorScreen component with error message and retry button that calls refetch()

acceptance_criteria.description: App launches and navigates to most recent - User opens the drawer - The conversation that was auto-navigated to is highlighted as active in the drawer list
acceptance_criteria.notes: switchConversation() is called to set activeConversationId in the hook

acceptance_criteria.description: App re-renders or hot-reloads - React re-render fires - Initial navigation logic does NOT run again (idempotent), no duplicate conversations created
acceptance_criteria.notes: hasInitialized.current ref prevents duplicate execution on re-renders and React 18 Strict Mode

key_implementations.initialization_logic: useEffect with hasInitialized ref to run once on app launch
key_implementations.navigation: Uses router.replace() to prevent back-stack issues (not router.navigate)
key_implementations.loading_state: InitialLoadingScreen with ActivityIndicator while determining initial conversation
key_implementations.error_state: InitialErrorScreen with retry button calling refetch()
key_implementations.most_recent_selection: Selects conversations[0] which is already sorted by updated_at
key_implementations.conversation_creation: Calls createConversation() when conversations array is empty

summary: Implemented app-open-to-most-recent-conversation logic in the drawer layout. On app launch, the app now shows a loading screen, then either navigates to the most recent conversation (by updated_at) or creates a new 'New Chat' conversation if none exist. Uses router.replace() to avoid back-stack issues and hasInitialized ref to prevent duplicate execution.
